data.body:
CONTAINER review (G4 — child consistency), parent c81c-cbcd-17a0-43d2 (plan-review fan-out optimization). Reviewed THIS child (S1) WHOLE against the complete sibling roster (S2 c6e5, S3 ba7e, S4 98c6, S5 1762, S6 da34) and the parent. Checked all 7 cross-child interaction modes. NO G4 FINDINGS.

Affirmations:
- Implicit shared state / consumer impact: S1 owns the runner.py cache flags + result.usage()/cache-token observability at the SHARED runner seam (used by passes.py and the engine RunnerAgentStep). S3 (ba7e) and S2 (c6e5) are downstream consumers — both assert cache_read_input_tokens > 0 on repeats. Producer→consumer is DAG-ordered (S1 blocks both per the dep links). Anthropic gating (resolved.startswith("anthropic")) protects existing openai/gemini consumers of the shared runner; no RunRequest content-list change protects the structured-output retry path.
- Conflicting assumptions: no sibling assumes the user-message CachePoint path. The parent Remediation ("cache the SYSTEM PROMPT … no RunRequest content-list change") supersedes the earlier CachePoint framing, and S2's de-risk E3 explicitly reconciles that the container {{plan}} is STABLE (S1's domain) vs generic gate prompts' volatile {{ticket_context}} (S2's domain). No contradiction.
- Dependency gap / ordering: S1 is foundational (no upstream deps); S2/S3 declare dependence. Byte-stability (S1 AC) is the precondition S3's warm-then-fan-out relies on, confirmed by S3 de-risk E2 (cold-concurrent => 3 writes/0 reads; warm-first => 0 writes/3 reads). Correctly ordered, no gap.
- Scope overlap: S1 fences out S2/S3/S4/S5 and each fences S1 reciprocally. Shared file runner.py is split cleanly — S1 owns the cache-flag mechanism; S2 owns RunnerAgentStep prefix-wiring/volatile-data relocation. No overlap.
- Residual references: no sibling carries a contract against a design S1 removed.

Non-G4 note (intra-child editorial only, below the cross-child/high-confidence threshold): the child TITLE still reads "CachePoint + anthropic_cache_*" while the body explicitly REJECTS the user-message CachePoint in favor of system-prompt caching. Stale title label, not a cross-child inconsistency; reads benignly under the ANTI-FP filter. (Already noted on a prior pass; recorded for editorial cleanup, not a finding.)
